pattern: Counting independent choices per position reduces to fast modular exponentiation of two base counts raised to the number of even and odd slots.
hints: Each position's choice is independent of the others, so the total is a product of per-position option counts.
Even indices have 5 valid digits and odd indices have 4; count how many of each kind of index a length-n string has.
The answer is pow(5, ceil(n/2)) times pow(4, floor(n/2)) modulo 1000000007; use fast exponentiation since n can be huge.

edge_inputs.description: smallest n, only one even slot
two digits, one even one odd slot
anchor example fifty digits triggers modulo
odd length where even slots exceed odd slots by one

stress.description: random large n across the allowed range
